title: Plan a Stage 8 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 8 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 8 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15, 16) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 8 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: Round 147,204 to the nearest 10.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: 147204 rounds to 147,200 — look at the ones digit.

questions.question: In the number 56,011, what is the value of the digit 5?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: 5 is in the ten thousands place, so it is worth 50,000.

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 4 = 19.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: If x = 6, what is the value of 3x + 2?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Which number is greater: 523,347 or 163,339?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Which number is greater: 957,658 or 853,486?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Which fraction is larger: 1/2 or 1/4?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: Halves are bigger than quarters, so 1/2 is larger than 1/4.

questions.question: What is 1/2 of 40?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: How many sides does a pentagon have?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: How many sides does a triangle have?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Here is some data: 12, 5, 3, 4, 9. What is the largest value?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Here is some data: 15, 16, 10, 3, 8, 15. Which value appears most often (the mode)?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: 15 appears more than any other value, so it is the mode.

questions.question: Work out 5/8 × 4/15.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, using the Unit 7 methods.
questions.explanation: Cancel 5 with 15 (both ÷ 5) and 4 with 8 (both ÷ 4): this gives 1/2 × 1/3 = 1/6. Cross-cancelling meant no large numbers and no simplifying at the end.

questions.question: Work out 0.8 + 0.6.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: Add the tenths: 8 tenths + 6 tenths = 14 tenths = 1.4.

questions.question: How many degrees are there in a full turn?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: How many sides does a pentagon have?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 5 = 18.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: If x = 5, what is the value of 7x + 5?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Which fraction is larger: 1/2 or 1/4?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: Halves are bigger than quarters, so 1/2 is larger than 1/4.

questions.question: Write 0.5 as a fraction.
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Here is some data: 18, 4, 8, 20, 6. What is the range?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Here is some data: 5, 7, 8, 14, 1. What is the range?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: 2/4 simplifies to 1/2, so they are equivalent.

questions.question: What is 25% of 150?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Here is some data: 4, 1, 12, 18, 17. What is the largest value?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: A tally shows these counts: 18, 1, 10, 11, 9. What is the total?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: How many sides does a pentagon have?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: A rectangle is 16 cm wide and 15 cm tall. What is its area in square centimetres?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: A rectangle is 11 cm wide and 15 cm tall. What is its area in square centimetres?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.

questions.question: Here is some data: 11, 6, 17, 1, 18, 11. Which value appears most often (the mode)?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
questions.explanation: 11 appears more than any other value, so it is the mode.

questions.question: Here is some data: 10, 8, 6, 17, 13. What is the range?
questions.hint: Work it out step by step, then check each option.
